intro: Vanhouttea salviifolia, also known as Brazilian mint, is a type of flowering plant native to Brazil, belonging to the family Lamiaceae. The leaves of Vanhouttea salviifolia have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, headaches, and inflammation, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant is rich in bioactive constituents such as vanillin, limonene, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Vanhouttea salviifolia include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are often used to calm digestive issues and promote relaxation.
